channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: In pictures: Marine Le Pen’s political rise and frustrated presidential ambitions
item.description: French far-right figurehead Marine Le Pen could see her 40-year political career come to an end on Tuesday if French courts rule that she is guilty of embezzling public funds and ineligible to stand for the presidency in 2027. FRANCE 24 takes a look at how she rose to prominence in France and brought her National Rally party into mainstream politics.
item.dc:creator: Romain BRUNET

item.title: How are the millions attending Khamenei's funeral remembering the late ayatollah?
item.description: The funeral procession for Iran's late supreme leader Ali Khamenei has begun in Tehran. Millions are expected to attend, possibly leading to crowds that could rival those that turned out for his predecessor nearly four decades ago. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah went to the procession and tells us why so many admired the late ayatollah.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Greece's Thessaloniki threatened by spreading wildfire
item.description: A fast-moving wildfire broke out in Greece's second largest city on July 4, forcing residents of three small suburbs north of the city to evacuate. The fire ignited a ravine in a mountainous area and spread quickly, with strong winds hampering efforts to contain the fire. According to the country's public broadcaster, a 76-year-old man was arrested on suspicion of starting the wildfire, as he allegedly ignited the blaze after his vehicle produced sparks.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: In Tehran, funeral attendees grieve the loss of supreme leader Ali Khamenei
item.description: In Iran, the funeral procession of the late supreme leader Ali Khamenei is moving through Tehran. Millions have turned out for the procession and the funeral of the ayatollah who had been lying in the capital's Grand Mosalla religious complex for two days. He will be buried in his home town on July 10.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Dissolution of Hamas's Gaza governing body won't bring 'significant change of life'
item.description: Hamas announced on July 7 the dissolution of the body that has governed the Gaza Strip for nearly two decades. The decision would clear the way for a technocratic committee to implement civilian rule. But questions remain. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky tells us more from Jerusalem.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: England hold on against Mexico while Norway oust Brazil
item.description: The Three Lions defied high altitude and a roaring home crowd to beat Mexico 3-2. Man of the match Jude Bellingham scored twice in the space of two minutes while Harry Kane's penalty kept England ahead of a fierce Mexico team. Meanwhile five-time champions Brazil were knocked-out by Norway, as Erling Haaland did what he does best to seal the win 2-1.
item.dc:creator: James VASINA

item.title: As a new heatwave looms, Europe falls for Chinese air conditioners
item.description: Hundreds of people besieged supermarkets in and around Paris, with scuffles and shouting matches breaking out as residents scrambled to get their hands on bargain air-cooling units before the next heatwave. With the hunt for affordability, Europeans are turning to Chinese AC units - despite trade tensions between Beijing and Brussels. FRANCE 24's Yena Lee tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Hamas dissolves Gaza governing body, clearing way for technocratic committee
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'Genuine admiration and emotion' in Tehran's streets as Iran mourns supreme leader
item.description: Vast crowds have gathered for the funeral procession of Iran's slain supreme leader Ali Khamenei in Tehran on July 6, with authorities estimating millions were on the streets. The final public tribute is expected to last around 10 hours. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah tells us more on what to know.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Venezuelans mobilise to deal with earthquakes' consequences: FRANCE 24 reports
item.description: As more than ten days have passed since Venezuela was struck by twin earthquakes, the death toll remains uncertain, with an official balance of at least 3,342 people killed. On the northern coastal part of the country, the hardest-hit region, survivors and volunteers from across the country are taking matters into their own hands : treating the injured, finding shelter, searching for the missing. FRANCE 24's Pascale Mariani and Maxime Pluvinet spoke to residents
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Hamas dissolves Gaza governing body, clearing way for technocratic committee
item.description: The Palestinian movement Hamas announced Monday that it had dissolved its governing body in the Gaza Strip after nearly 20 years in power, paving the way for a technocratic committee to administer the territory.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Moldova speeds up its membership process to the EU
item.description: An economically fragile country caught up in Russia’s hybrid war, Moldova is keen to make rapid progress for a quicker membership to the European Union. With the opening of the first chapter of accession negotiations, the EU launched its €1.9 billion investment plan in the country. With this plan, which is conditional on domestic structural reforms, Europe hopes to draw in businesses and drive change in the country.
item.dc:creator: Caroline DE CAMARET

item.title: Macron's visit to Syria confirms normalisation strategy of post-Assad government
item.description: Syria is expecting its first Western European head of state since new leader Ahmed al-Sharaa swept to power in 2024. A Syrian state news agency reported that France's Emmanuel Macron will visit to discuss strengthening bilateral relations. It wouldn't be the first time the two have met. In May 2025, Macron welcomed al-Sharaa to the Élysée Palace, where they discussed issues of security and Macron addressed the interreligious violence in Syria.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: At least 14 killed in Kyiv after second massive Russian attack
item.description: Kyiv has been struck by Russian missiles for the second time in less than a week, with at least 14 people killed. While Russia says it hit military-industrial targets and energy sites, the morning strikes hit residential areas in the early hours of the morning. FRANCE 24's Gulliver Cragg tells us more from Odesa.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'Like we won a finae': England savour dramatic win and other World Cup news
item.description: England has won a nerve-racking World Cup classic 3-2 against Mexico on July 7 to reach the quarterfinals, after Jude Bellingham scored two goals and Harry Kanes scored a penalty. Norway has also made history by reaching the round of 8 for the first time, after disqualifying Brazil. FRANCE 24's James Vasina tells us more on what to know from the latest on the World Cup.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Supporters stopped from attending Tour de France as wildfires ravage South of France
item.description: Excess heat and wildfires are affecting the Tour de France cycle race. The third stage of the race on July 7 will go near an area where wildfires have broken out in France's southwest. Local authorities and race organisers as a result have asked people not to go to the race route to cheer riders on.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Syria expecting Macron visit in first for Western head-of-state since Assad
item.description: Syria is expecting a visit by French President Emmanuel Macron, the first by a Western European head of state since Syria's new leader Ahmed al-Sharaa took power in 2024. Macron would be accompanied by a delegation "including investors and representatives of French companies". FRANCE 24's Matthew-Mary Caruchet tells us what we know.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Putin 'starting to understand he may not win' the war in Ukraine
item.description: Russia struck Ukraine's Kyiv region with ballistic missiles and drones on July 7, killing at least 11 people and wounding dozens on the eve of a NATO summit in Turkey. This comes as, within Russia, the war has come under growing criticism as Ukraine has ramped up strikes inside the country and Russian casualties keep growing. For FRANCE 24's Nick Holdsworth, these deadly attacks are a 'sign of desperation' as Putin is realising he might not win the war.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Kyiv struck by second deadly Russian attack in less than a week
item.description: Russia has launched another deadly barrage of strikes on Kyiv. According to Ukrainian authorities, at least fourteen people have been killed, just hours after President Volodomyr Zelensky warned another attack was imminent. Earlier on July 5 the city of Zaporizhzhia was also hit and Moscow claims it has captured the town of Kostyantynivka in the east of the country. Kyiv has dismissed the announcement.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Science should be developed away from being 'exclusive club' - Nobel prize winner to F24
item.description: Omar M. Yaghi grew up in the Jordanian capital Ammann in a family of Palestinian refugees. He moved to the United States at the age of 15 and, working his way through academia via Community College, obtained a PhD in chemistry in 1990. He has held professorships at the University of Michigan, UCLA and the University of California at Berkeley. In 2025 he was awarded the Nobel Prize in Chemistry, along with Susumu Kitagawa and Richard Robson, for his work on developing metal–organic frameworks. It was announced this week that Professor Yaghi is to join Tsinghua University in Beijing to lead a new AI-driven research centre.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Belgium granted right to appeal FIFA's Balogun decision
item.description: Belgium have been granted the right to appeal FIFA's decision to overturn US striker Folarin Balogun's suspension, The Athletic reported on Monday, which reportedly took place after an intervention from US President Donald Trump.
item.source: © Stephen Lam, San Francisco Chronicle via AP
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Protecting Corsican Scandola Nature Reserve amid tourism boom
item.description: Scandola, a UNESCO-listed nature reserve off the coast of Corsica, is attracting a growing number of visitors from around the world – drawn to its dramatic landscapes and rich marine life. However, protecting its unique wildlife and fragile marine ecosystem is becoming an increasingly delicate balancing act. Our colleagues at France 2 and FRANCE 24's Guillaume Gougeon report.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 2

item.title: Thousands evacuated in southwestern France as wildfire burns out of control
item.description: Ten thousand people in southwestern France were forced to evacuate due to a wildfire that is still burning and has already destroyed 4,600 hectares of forest, authorities said on Monday.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: US team’s Folarin Balogun free to play after Trump lobbies FIFA to remove ban
item.description: PRESS REVIEW – Monday, 6 July 2026: First, the French papers ready themselves for an upcoming verdict in far-right presidential candidate Marine Le Pen's embezzlement case. Next, the New York Times features a long read on the battle for the internet’s soul. Also, Folarin Balogun will play in Team USA’s next game after US President Donald Trump lobbied FIFA to remove the match suspension he received after being shown a red card in the previous game. Finally, the Belgians have become shrimp shelling world champions.
item.dc:creator: Emily BOYLE
